arts.ttl: Juventus appeals arbitration ruling in Cristiano Ronaldo case
arts.sum: Juventus have announced their decision to appeal the recent arbitration ruling regarding former player Cristiano Ronaldo. This development was revealed in the club’s annual financial report, which was approved by the Board of Directors. The financial statement, which showed a loss of €199.2 million and a decrease in financial debt, also addressed the ongoing legal dispute with the Portuguese superstar, now playing for Al-Nassr. The arbitration panel had partially accepted Ronaldo’s claim concerning the salary maneuver during his time at Juventus, resulting in the club being ordered to pay him approximately €9.8 million. arts.ttl: Douglas Costa: ‘Juventus will have to be patient with Douglas Luiz’
arts.sum: In a recent interview with Gazzetta dello Sport, former Juventus player Douglas Costa, now with Sydney FC, shared his thoughts on various topics related to Italian football and his former club. Costa began by discussing Douglas Luiz’s adaptation to Serie A, emphasizing the need for patience: “An adaptation period is normal when you arrive from another league. It happened to me too. I came from a top club like Bayern, but Serie A is a particular tournament. To give you an idea: a 1-0 win at Juve was like a 5-0 win at Bayern. arts.ttl: Genoa vs. Juventus: mission three points
arts.sum: Genoa remained unbeaten in their last three league matches against Juventus (W1 D2), after five defeats in a row; only once did the Grifone record a streak of four games without losing against the Bianconeri in Serie A: between February 1990 and October 1991. Juventus have drawn each of their last three league matches with a score of 0-0, the Bianconeri could draw four goalless games in a row for the first time in their history in Serie A.
